hooks.command: $j = $input | ConvertFrom-Json; $c = $j.tool_input.command; $hD = $c -match '--config\s+Debug'; $hR = $c -match '--config\s+Release'; if ($hD -xor $hR) { $null = $c -match 'cmake\s+--build\s+(\S+)'; $p = $matches[1]; @{hookSpecificOutput=@{hookEventName='PreToolUse';updatedInput=@{command="cmake --build $p --config Debug && cmake --build $p --config Release"}}} | ConvertTo-Json -Compress }

hooks.statusMessage: Copying artifacts to OneDrive Windows folder...
hooks.command: $data = $input | ConvertFrom-Json; if ($data.tool_response.exitCode -eq 0) { $dst = 'D:\Andy Tuke\Music - Music Software\TDP\Windows\'; Copy-Item 'd:\Dev\mu\build\mu-clid_artefacts\Release\Standalone\mu-Clid.exe' $dst -Force 2>$null; Copy-Item 'd:\Dev\mu\build\mu-clid_artefacts\Release\VST3\mu-Clid.vst3' $dst -Recurse -Force 2>$null; Copy-Item 'd:\Dev\mu\build\mu-clid_artefacts\Release\CLAP\mu-Clid.clap' $dst -Force 2>$null }

hooks.statusMessage: Scanning build output for compiler warnings...
hooks.prompt:
Scan the cmake build output in this JSON for compiler warnings: $ARGUMENTS

Look for warning lines in the tool_response output field. Warning patterns: ': warning:' (GCC/Clang) and 'warning C' (MSVC). Skip note/continuation lines that have no warning code. If no warnings are found, stop immediately and do nothing.

For each unique warning (deduplicate by file+line+warning-code):
1. Read the source file at the indicated path to understand the context around the warning line.
2. Determine what fix is needed and why.
3. Read d:\Dev\mu\backlog.md to find the current highest issue number in the Open section.
4. Prepend a new Open issue at the very top of the Open section: - [ ] #NNN: Compiler warning: <brief description> (<filename>:<line>)

Never add a duplicate if the same warning is already described in an existing Open backlog entry.

hooks.statusMessage: Checking first build of day for oversight trigger...
